skill.name: uv-package-manager
skill.description: Guide Python teams through uv project setup, dependency management, virtual environments, lockfiles, migration, and CI workflows.
skill.summary: Use uv for fast Python dependency management, virtual environments, lockfiles, and project workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly documentation examples for uv commands, package installation, Git dependencies, and project configuration. The remote installer examples use pipe-to-shell and PowerShell iex patterns, so publication is acceptable only with a warning about reviewing installer scripts and trusted sources.

medium_findings.title: Remote Installer Piped To Shell
medium_findings.description: The installation guidance downloads scripts from astral.sh and immediately executes them with sh or PowerShell iex. This is a real supply-chain risk if users copy the commands without reviewing the script source, even though it points to the official uv installer.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell and iex patterns are present in installation examples. The domain appears legitimate for uv, so this is risky guidance rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Broad External Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill contains many shell, Docker, CI, Git, and uv command examples. These commands are expected for a package-manager skill, but users should review generated commands before execution because they modify environments, lockfiles, packages, and project state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible throughout SKILL.md and align with the skill purpose. Risk depends on whether an assistant executes adapted commands without user review.

medium_findings.title: Hidden File And Shell Profile Modifications
medium_findings.description: The documentation references .venv, .python-version, .vscode settings, uv cache directories, and appending PATH changes to ~/.bashrc. These are normal Python workflow files, but they can alter interpreter selection and shell startup behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden file paths and shell profile edit are directly shown. They are legitimate uv workflow guidance but carry configuration risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation And Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to uv documentation, the uv GitHub repository, Astral resources, or placeholder GitHub package examples. They are expected references for this skill and do not show data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible as documentation links and illustrative dependency sources. I found no evidence that they transmit local data or credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography hits appear to match unrelated documentation text, such as project description fields and resource links. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines do not contain cryptographic code or instructions. The static pattern is a false positive caused by ordinary documentation text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Match Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static system reconnaissance hit maps to best-practice text about using uv run. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, or unauthorized system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a usage recommendation, not a reconnaissance command. The surrounding context is a best-practices summary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Installer
dangerous_patterns.description: The macOS/Linux install example pipes a downloaded installer directly into sh.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command explicitly downloads a script and executes it through sh. This is a known risky installation pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: PowerShell Download And Execute
dangerous_patterns.description: The Windows install example downloads a remote PowerShell installer and executes it through iex.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command explicitly combines remote retrieval with iex execution. This is a known risky installation pattern.

content.value_statement: Python dependency workflows can be slow and inconsistent across local, CI, and container environments. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply uv patterns for faster installs, lockfiles, virtual environments, and migrations.
actual_capabilities: Explains uv installation options for macOS, Linux, Windows, pip, Homebrew, and cargo.
Guides project initialization with uv init, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, and .python-version.
Shows dependency workflows for add, remove, upgrade, sync, lock, and requirements export.
Covers virtual environments, uv run, Python version pinning, and interpreter installation.
Provides CI, Docker, pre-commit, VS Code, monorepo, and migration workflow examples.
Includes troubleshooting guidance for PATH, dependency conflicts, cache issues, and stale lockfiles.
limitations: It is documentation-only and does not verify the latest uv command behavior at runtime.
Some install examples execute remote scripts and require user trust in the source.
Examples use placeholder package and repository names that must be adapted safely.
It does not replace security review of third-party packages or Git dependencies.

use_cases.title: Start a Python service
use_cases.description: Create a new project, pin Python, add runtime and development dependencies, and run tests through uv.

use_cases.title: Modernize dependency workflows
use_cases.description: Move an existing pip, pip-tools, or Poetry project toward uv sync, uv lock, and reproducible installs.

use_cases.title: Speed up CI and containers
use_cases.description: Apply uv cache, frozen lockfile, GitHub Actions, and Docker patterns for faster repeatable builds.

prompt_templates.title: Create a uv project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new Python project with uv. Include the project files, dependency groups, virtual environment workflow, and first test command.

prompt_templates.title: Convert from pip
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my requirements-based Python project and propose a uv migration plan. Explain lockfile handling, commands to run, and files to commit.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI with uv
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CI workflow for a Python project using uv. Include Python setup, dependency caching, frozen installs, tests, linting, and failure checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit uv dependency strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this Python repository's uv setup for reproducibility, dependency grouping, workspace structure, Docker usage, and security risks.

output_examples.input: Set up a new FastAPI project with uv.
output_examples.output: A concise project setup plan with runtime dependencies, development tools, Python version pinning, lockfile steps, and test commands.

output_examples.input: Migrate this requirements.txt workflow to uv.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering project initialization, dependency import, lockfile creation, validation, and files that should be committed.

output_examples.input: Make our Python CI faster with uv.
output_examples.output: A CI design that uses uv setup, cache reuse, frozen dependency installation, linting, tests, and clear failure points.

best_practices: Review remote installer scripts before executing curl or PowerShell download commands.
Commit uv.lock and use frozen sync commands in CI for reproducible builds.
Use uv run and explicit Python pinning to avoid accidental interpreter drift.
anti_patterns: Do not execute generated package commands without checking package names and sources.
Do not depend on unpinned Git references for production dependencies.
Do not modify shell profile files unless the PATH change is necessary and reviewed.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan and explain uv workflows for Python projects, dependencies, virtual environments, lockfiles, CI, Docker, and migrations.

faq.question: Can it run uv commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides command guidance. Command execution depends on the host AI tool and should require user review.

faq.question: Is the remote installer guidance safe?
faq.answer: It uses official uv installer URLs, but piping remote scripts to a shell is risky. Review the script before running it.

faq.question: Can it replace Poetry or pip-tools?
faq.answer: It can guide migration from those tools, but each repository should be tested before removing the old workflow.

faq.question: Does it support CI and Docker workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for GitHub Actions, frozen lockfile installs, uv caching, and Docker image patterns.

faq.question: Does it audit package security?
faq.answer: No. It explains uv workflows but does not scan third-party packages or verify dependency trust.
